majorEmployers.name: Riverwind Casino
majorEmployers.notes: Owned and operated by the Chickasaw Nation; one of the largest employers in McClain County

majorEmployers.name: Newcastle Casino
majorEmployers.notes: Owned and operated by the Chickasaw Nation; 112,000 square-foot facility

majorEmployers.name: Walmart Supercenter

uniqueFacts: McClain County was originally part of Curtis County in the proposed state of Sequoyah before Oklahoma achieved statehood in 1907.
The county was named for Charles M. McClain, a member of the Oklahoma Constitutional Convention and an early resident of Purcell.
Newcastle is the largest city in McClain County, while Purcell serves as the county seat.
The South Canadian River forms the entire northern border of the L-shaped county.
McClain County is the second fastest growing county in the Oklahoma City metropolitan area, with nearly 21% growth from 2010 to 2020.
processServingNotes: McClain County is part of the 21st Judicial District of Oklahoma. The courthouse is located at 121 N. 2nd Street in Purcell, with hours Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM.
The Chickasaw Nation has significant tribal jurisdiction throughout McClain County, which may affect legal proceedings involving tribal citizens or tribal land.
Major population centers include Newcastle in the northwest, Purcell in the center, and Blanchard in the northwest, requiring process servers to cover a wide geographic area.
heroLead: Located in the heart of south-central Oklahoma, McClain County is one of the fastest-growing counties in the Oklahoma City metro area, encompassing 580 square miles and home to over 48,000 residents across vibrant communities like Newcastle, Purcell, and Blanchard. From its historic Chickasaw Nation heritage to its thriving gaming, education, and healthcare industries, McClain County combines small-town charm with modern economic vitality. Whether you need process serving at the Purcell courthouse, a business in Newcastle, or a residence in Goldsby, Just Legal Solutions delivers reliable, efficient service throughout McClain County. Contact us today at (539) 367-6832 or visit our /contact page to get started.

faqs.question: Where is the McClain County courthouse located and what are its hours?
faqs.answer: The McClain County Courthouse is located at 121 N. 2nd Street, Suite 231, Purcell, Oklahoma 73080. The courthouse is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, closed on weekends and state holidays. You can reach the court clerk's office at 405-527-3221 for questions about case filings or to confirm office hours before visiting. McClain County is part of Oklahoma's 21st Judicial District.

faqs.question: What is the population of McClain County and how fast is it growing?
faqs.answer: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, McClain County had an estimated population of 48,434 in 2024. The county has experienced significant growth, increasing by nearly 21% between 2010 and 2020, making it one of the fastest-growing counties in Oklahoma. Much of this growth is attributed to its location within the Oklahoma City metropolitan area and its popularity as a suburban community. For process serving needs in this growing community, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: Which tribal nation has jurisdiction in McClain County?
faqs.answer: The Chickasaw Nation has tribal jurisdiction throughout McClain County. The Chickasaw Nation is a federally recognized tribal government headquartered in Ada, Oklahoma, with jurisdiction spanning 13 counties in south-central Oklahoma, including all of McClain County. This jurisdiction may impact legal proceedings involving tribal citizens or land within the county's boundaries.

faqs.question: What are the major cities and towns in McClain County?
faqs.answer: The largest city in McClain County is Newcastle, located in the northwestern part of the county with approximately 10,000-15,000 residents. Purcell serves as the county seat and is home to about 6,500 people. Other incorporated communities include Blanchard, Goldsby, Dibble, Washington, Wayne, Cole, Byars, and Rosedale. Each of these communities has its own municipal court in addition to the county district court in Purcell.

faqs.question: Who are the largest employers in McClain County?
faqs.answer: The largest employers in McClain County include the Chickasaw Nation's Riverwind Casino in Goldsby and Newcastle Casino in Newcastle, which together employ over 2,000 people. Other major employers include Purcell Municipal Hospital, Mid-America Technology Center in Wayne, Newcastle Public Schools, Blanchard Public Schools, and the Walmart Supercenter in Purcell. The county's economy is also supported by agribusiness, manufacturing, and retail trade.

faqs.question: When was McClain County founded and who was it named after?
faqs.answer: McClain County was established at Oklahoma statehood in 1907. It was originally part of Curtis County in the proposed state of Sequoyah. The county was named for Charles M. McClain, a member of the 1906 Oklahoma Constitutional Convention and an early resident of Purcell. The county encompasses 580 square miles of land and water in south-central Oklahoma. For assistance with legal document service in McClain County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.
